narrative.description: Terraria is a 2D sandbox action game combining exploration, crafting, building, and boss progression across a procedurally generated world.
narrative.core_loop: Mine resources and craft gear, build bases, fight escalating bosses to unlock new materials and NPCs, and explore surface, underground, and hardmode biomes.

mechanics.map_notes: In Terraria, minimal UI encourages discovery of landmarks and secrets across vast terrain.
mechanics.expression: In Terraria, minimal UI encourages discovery of landmarks and secrets across vast terrain.

mechanics.map_notes: In Terraria, a progression gate where a fixed collection target must be met before an upgrade or skill point becomes available, tying advancement directly to measurable progress.
mechanics.expression: In Terraria, a progression gate where a fixed collection target must be met before an upgrade or skill point becomes available, tying advancement directly to measurable progress.

mechanics.map_notes: In Terraria, the primary offensive loop uses close-range melee strikes rather than projectile weapons.
mechanics.expression: In Terraria, the primary offensive loop uses close-range melee strikes rather than projectile weapons.

mechanics.map_notes: In Terraria, players collect currency through gameplay and exchange it at vendors for permanent upgrades, creating economic progression choices.
mechanics.expression: In Terraria, players collect currency through gameplay and exchange it at vendors for permanent upgrades, creating economic progression choices.

mechanics.map_notes: In Terraria, a progression structure in which access to subsequent content areas, abilities, or game states is conditionally locked behind the defeat of a designated boss.
mechanics.expression: In Terraria, a progression structure in which access to subsequent content areas, abilities, or game states is conditionally locked behind the defeat of a designated boss.

design_intent.player_fantasy: Experience Terraria through its core sandbox loop.
